compiled_state.title: As a developer, when multiple competing branches exist for a ticket, the system automatically selects the best implementation
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Extends the single-branch resume logic from Story C-Foundation to handle the case where multiple unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments exist from distinct branches on the same ticket. Selects the best implementation using a deterministic tiebreak cascade.

**Why**: Parallel sub-agent runs (race condition or deliberate re-run) can produce competing implementations — without tiebreak logic, the orchestrator cannot automatically choose and must either block or pick arbitrarily, requiring manual intervention.

**Scope**:
- IN: Detecting multiple unmatched starts from distinct branches on the same ticket; ranking branches by the cascade: (1) count of verbatim markdown task-list criterion matches (`- [ ]` or `- [x]` items from ticket description) appearing in each branch's diff, (2) test-gate-status artifact (passed > failed > absent), (3) fewest merge conflicts, (4) most recent start timestamp; merging the winning branch and discarding the rest
- OUT: Single-branch recovery (Story C-Foundation); comment write infrastructure (Stories A/B); de-duplication of multiple starts from the same branch (treated as one branch, de-duped by branch name)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, when two or more unmatched WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comments from distinct branches exist on the same ticket, sprint and fix-bug apply the tiebreak cascade — (1) count verbatim markdown task-list criterion matches in each branch's git diff, (2) compare test-gate-status artifacts if criteria counts are equal, (3) count merge conflicts as the next tiebreaker, (4) fall back to most-recent start timestamp — then merge the winning branch into the session branch and discard the losers
  ← Satisfies: "When two competing unmerged implementations exist on a ticket, the orchestrator selects the better one using this priority order: (1) whichever implementation's diff addresses more acceptance criteria items... (2) test-gate-status artifact as tiebreaker... (3) fewest merge conflicts as the final tiebreaker; (4) most recently started by WORKTREE_TRACKING:start comment timestamp as the ultimate fallback."

- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] AC counting requires parsing the ticket's markdown description for `- [ ]`/`- [x]` items and checking verbatim text presence in git diff output — unit tests must cover edge cases: no task-list items in description, identical AC counts across branches, missing test-gate-status artifact, all tiebreakers tied (timestamp fallback)
- [Maintainability] The tiebreak cascade is a deterministic algorithm — encode it as a shared helper function with clearly labeled tiebreak stages so the selection logic is auditable and independently testable
- [Implementation] Multiple starts from the same branch name are de-duplicated: treat them as a single candidate using the most recent timestamp

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
